name: Tempeh Stuffed Peppers
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Olive or toasted sesame oil
1 small Tomato, pureed to yield 1/2 cup
4 medium Red or green bell peppers
12 oz Soy or 3-5 grain tempeh, grated
2 tbsp Dark barley or red miso, tamari or shoyu
1/4 cup Whole grain bread crumbs, cracker crumbs, or rolled oats
1/4 cup Cooked beans (black, pinto, azuki, anazadi, or kidney)
2 tsp Dried garlic flakes OR 1-2 cloves Garlic, minced and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive or toasted sesame oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot.
2. Sauté onion briefly until softened.
3. Add garlic and cook for an additional 30 seconds.
4. Add chopped tomatoes and tomato puree, then sauerkraut.
5. Stir to combine and simmer on low heat for 5-10 minutes.
6. Cut the tops off the bell peppers and scoop out the insides, discarding seeds.
7. In a mixing bowl, combine filling ingredients: tempeh, miso, bread crumbs, cooked beans, and seasonings.
8. Mix with your hands to distribute herbs and spices evenly.
9. Divide the filling into 4 portions and stuff each bell pepper.
10. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
11. Place the stuffed peppers upright in the pot with the sauce.
12. Cover and bake for 10 minutes on top of the stove.
13. Transfer the pot to the oven and bake for another 30 minutes, without removing the lid.
14. Remove the lid and continue baking for an additional 20-15 minutes.
15. Serve hot in bowls, topped with sauce. Accompany with whole grain bread or tortillas and salad.
Variation: Replace fresh tomatoes and tomato puree with 2 cups finely grated carrot or minced winter squash, adding 1 cup of water or soup stock.
